name: Unlockable Abilities
description: A progression rule that grants new verbs, powers, passives, or interaction options after requirements are met.
design_notes: Unlockable abilities change what the player can do, so they need stronger safeguards than simple stat rewards. Each ability should have ability_id, unlock_requirement_id, grant timing, tutorial readiness, save migration, and rollback rules if a prerequisite is removed.
design_purpose: Introduce new verbs or strategic options at a pace the player can learn.
Reward milestones with meaningful capability changes rather than only larger numbers.
Gate traversal, combat, crafting, or social options behind clear progression requirements.
player_fantasy: The player feels their character or account is learning new ways to act in the world.

parameters.name: ability_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier for the ability granted to the player.

parameters.name: unlock_requirement_id
parameters.description: Requirement that must be satisfied before the ability is granted or purchasable.

parameters.name: grant_scope
parameters.description: Persistence scope that decides whether the ability survives resets, death, or gear changes.

parameters.name: tutorial_prompt_id
parameters.description: Optional onboarding prompt associated with the new ability.

parameters.name: retroactive_unlock_flag
parameters.description: Whether existing saves that already meet the requirement receive the ability automatically.

edge_cases: An ability unlocks while the player is airborne, in combat, or in a menu and should not steal input unexpectedly.
A removed prerequisite must decide whether to keep, refund, or revoke already granted abilities.
Abilities used for traversal can soft-lock saves if revoked while the player is behind a gate.
Multiplayer games need to decide whether ability unlocks are predicted locally or confirmed by the server.
common_bugs: Ability UI appears unlocked but the input binding or action map is not enabled.
Save data stores display names instead of ability_id, breaking migration and localization.
Retroactive unlocks grant the same ability multiple times because grant transaction IDs are not tracked.
Revoking an equipment-granted ability leaves cooldowns, summons, or passive modifiers active.
balancing_notes: New verbs should arrive when the game has space to teach and test them, not only when the XP curve needs a reward.
Traversal abilities can invalidate old level layouts, so gating and backtracking should be planned together.
Account-wide ability unlocks need special care because they can shorten or trivialize early-game pacing.
accessibility_notes: Provide input remapping and tutorial review for newly unlocked actions.
Avoid locking required accessibility assists behind progression; capability unlocks should not gate basic playability.

implementation_notes.unity: Grant ability_id through a central ability service that updates action maps, cooldown state, tutorials, and save data after unlock_requirement_id validation succeeds.
implementation_notes.godot: Store ability definitions as Resources and keep owned ability IDs in progression_state; emit signals after grants so UI and input maps refresh from committed state.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Gameplay Ability System or a progression component to grant abilities authoritatively; ability specs should be added or removed from committed unlock state, not UI previews.
implementation_notes.web: Keep ability definitions in JSON and owned ability IDs in saved state; use pure requirement checks for UI visibility and commit ability grants with transaction IDs.

example_games.title: Metroid
example_games.note: Uses ability unlocks to open new traversal and combat options.

example_games.title: Celeste
example_games.note: Introduces and teaches new movement capabilities through progression and level design.
